rulings.Case Title: TRACEY THOMPSON VS. JOHN STEWART CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Bifurcate And Sever Action To Try Equitable Claims First Without A Jury
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY Motion To Bifurcate And Sever Action To Try Equitable Claims First Without A Jury is DENIED without prejudice to be raised before the trial judge. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRACEY THOMPSON VS. JOHN STEWART CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues is DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain its burden and show that there are no triable issues of fact as to the ongoing failure to reasonably accommodate the request for in-unit washer and dryer hook up; that such ongoing failure to accommodate has a remedy at law (as opposed to money damages for past failures); and that there is no risk of irreparable harm for such ongoing failure to accommodate. Furthermore, there is a triable issue of fact regarding the reasonableness of defendant's attempt to accommodate which resulted in Plaintiff using her sink to wash her clothes since 2015 to present, and presumably in the future. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JENNY SAI-YUN SHU CHANG ET AL VS. SARA SHU CHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2020 line 3. PLAINTIFF JENNY SHU CHANG, STELLA SHU LOO, PHILLIP SHU Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement is DENIED as requested. Plaintiff moves for entry of judgment in its favor and against the defendant. Plaintiff points to no provision in the settlement agreement that would support an entry of judgment in such form. While Plaintiff cites to Hines (in reply) Plaintiff is not seeking "to enter a judgment reflecting all of the material terms of settlement that have yet to be fully performed." Nothing in CCP 664.6 compels entry of the judgment in the form requested by Plaintiff in absence of evidence that Plaintiff is in fact entitled to a judgment in its favor pursuant to such agreement. Plaintiff's second argument raised in reply also does not warrant such result. CCP 664.6 allows the Court to retain jurisdiction per agreement of the parties, a simple request to the Court to do so would accomplish the goal, while an entry of judgment in favor of Plaintiff would be contrary to the parties' express agreement. =(501/CFH)
